ucs.title: [LLM] APT36 (Transparent Tribe) Crimson RAT delivery via Indian-gov themed maldocs spawning persistence
ucs.description: Hunts the characteristic Transparent Tribe / Mythic Leopard chain where an Office or archive lure themed around Indian MoD / Army / Kavach 2FA is opened, the parent Office/WinRAR process spawns a .NET binary masquerading as a system utility, and that binary beacons to a dynamic-DNS C2 (ddns.net, hopto.org, sytes.net) — the signature pattern for Crimson RAT, ObliqueRAT, and Poseidon payloads attributed to G0134 / COPPER FIELDSTONE.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child values(Processes.process_path) as path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","WinRAR.exe","7zG.exe","explorer.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("*.exe","*.scr","*.pif") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=process_name "(?i)(kavach|svchosts|dwm|winrm|update|microsoft|msoffice|csrss|conhost|chrome|adobe|jli|jvm)" | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port values(All_Traffic.app) as resolved_dom from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("*.ddns.net","*.hopto.org","*.sytes.net","*.no-ip.*","*.bounceme.net","*.zapto.org") by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name process_name process_path c2_ip c2_port resolved_dom
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","winrar.exe","7zg.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let apt36DynDns = dynamic([".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net",".bounceme.net",".zapto.org",".myftp.biz",".no-ip.org",".no-ip.biz"]);
let kavachLures = @"(?i)(kavach|moddef|moddefence|indianarmy|cdacmumbai|drdo|nic|gov\.in|mea|rashtrapati|mod\.gov)";
let susChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (suspiciousParents)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Public\")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".pif"
| extend masquerade = iff(FileName matches regex @"(?i)^(svchost|svchosts|dwm|csrss|conhost|winlogon|services|update|chrome|adobe|jli|jvm|msoffice|microsoft).*\.(exe|scr|pif)$", true, false)
| extend kavachLure = iff(InitiatingProcessCommandLine matches regex kavachLures or ProcessCommandLine matches regex kavachLures, true, false)
| where masquerade or kavachLure
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, SHA256;
let beacons = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (apt36DynDns) or RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemoteUrl has_any (apt36DynDns)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, NetInitiatingProcessFileName=InitiatingProcessFileName, NetSHA256=InitiatingProcessSHA256;
susChildren
| join kind=inner (beacons) on DeviceName
| where (NetTime - Timestamp) between (0min .. 60min)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort, SHA256
ucs.rationale: Transparent Tribe / APT36 has consistently (per Talos 2021, Kaspersky 2020, Cisco/Zscaler 2022-2024) paired Indian-government / Kavach 2FA themed maldocs and trojanized installers with Crimson RAT, ObliqueRAT, and Poseidon payloads dropped to AppData/ProgramData and named after Windows utilities, beaconing to free dynamic-DNS providers — they almost never own dedicated registered C2 infrastructure

ucs.title: [LLM] APT36 cross-platform payload: weaponized .desktop / ELF lures targeting BOSS Linux & Indian defense endpoints
ucs.description: Transparent Tribe pivoted in 2023-2024 to ELF/Go (Poseidon, GLOBSHELL) and weaponized .desktop launcher files targeting BOSS Linux (Indian government distro) and macOS, plus CapraRAT-laced APKs. This UC catches the rare event of a Linux/macOS endpoint executing a .desktop or ELF dropped from a browser/mail client and reaching out to APT36-style staging infrastructure — a tradecraft footprint very few actors share.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.desktop" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.elf" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.AppImage" OR Filesystem.file_path="*/Downloads/*" AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("*Army*","*MoD*","*Defence*","*Kavach*","*BOSS*","*OFB*","*DRDO*","*PaySlip*")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process) as exec_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("chmod","bash","sh","python3","gio","xdg-open") AND Processes.process IN ("*+x*","*chmod 7*","*.desktop*","*Exec=*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Web.url) as url values(Web.dest) as web_dest from datamodel=Web where (Web.url="*.gov.in.*" OR Web.url="*govin-*" OR Web.url="*-gov-in*" OR Web.url="*ministryofdefence*" OR Web.url="*indiandefence*" OR Web.url="*kavach-*" OR Web.url="*bossupdate*" OR Web.url="*.ddns.net" OR Web.url="*.hopto.org") by Web.src | rename Web.src as dest] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | table firstTime dest user file_name file_path process_name parent_process_name exec_cmd url web_dest
ucs.kql:
let apt36LureKeywords = dynamic(["army","mod","defence","kavach","boss","ofb","drdo","payslip","posting","transfer","dgmo","meadg"]);
let apt36DomainPatterns = dynamic(["gov-in","govin-","-gov-in","ministryofdefence","indiandefence","kavach-","bossupdate",".ddns.net",".hopto.org",".sytes.net"]);
let linuxMacDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where DeviceName has_any ("linux","boss","ubuntu","rhel","mac") or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("firefox","chrome","thunderbird","evolution","safari")
| where FileName endswith ".desktop" or FileName endswith ".elf" or FileName endswith ".AppImage" or FileName endswith ".sh"
   or (FolderPath has "/Downloads/" and (FileName has_any (apt36LureKeywords)))
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256;
let permFlip = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("chmod","bash","sh","python3","gio","xdg-open","gtk-launch")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("+x",".desktop","Exec=","chmod 7")
| project ExecTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName;
let stagingHits = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any (apt36DomainPatterns)
| project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetInitiatingProcessFileName=InitiatingProcessFileName;
linuxMacDrops
| join kind=inner (permFlip) on DeviceName
| where (ExecTime - DropTime) between (0min .. 30min)
| join kind=inner (stagingHits) on DeviceName
| where (NetTime - DropTime) between (-30min .. 60min)
| project DropTime, ExecTime, NetTime, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, SHA256
| sort by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: BlackBerry, Volexity and CYFIRMA reporting (2023-2024) documented Transparent Tribe specifically targeting India's BOSS Linux distribution with .desktop launcher payloads (Poseidon/GLOBSHELL) and gov-in lookalike staging domains — a vector almost no other tracked actor uses at scale. Catching a Linux/macOS endpoint dropping a .desktop or ELF with Indian-defense lure keywords, immediately chmod'd e
